Tests the behavior of the `with` statement in JavaScript using the `test` function.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the [`test`](<#10-006test>) function. The [`test`](<#10-006test>) function takes an argument `arg` and uses a `with` statement to return the `arg` itself. The `assert` statement checks three conditions: that calling [`test`](<#10-006test>) with a string returns the same string, that calling [`test`](<#10-006test>) with an object returns an object, and that calling [`test`](<#10-006test>) with an object containing a property `arg` with the value "hello" returns "hello". This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on testing the behavior of the [`test`](<#10-006test>) function with different types of input.

### test<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/12/12.10/12.10-006.test}} -->

Returns the input argument `arg` after executing a `with` statement on it.
- **Inputs**:
    - `arg`: The input argument that the function processes, which can be of any type.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Executes a `with` statement using the input `arg`.
    - Returns the input `arg` directly.
- **Output**: The same value as the input argument `arg`.
